data.title: What's in store for Wall Street in 2026? SA analysts weigh in
data.description:
With just one month remaining in the 2025 trading year, Wall Street has delivered a relatively strong performance, despite a brief stumble in November.

Looking at the broader landscape, all major U.S. equity benchmarks remain firmly positive for the year. The question now is: what comes next in 2026? 

Outlined below are some of the viewpoints that Seeking Alpha's analysts hold about what is in store for 2026:

SA analyst James Foord echoed: “2026 is poised for a rare alignment of policy, liquidity, and sentiment, supporting a bullish outlook for equities and risk assets,” in 2026: A Rare Alignment Of Policy, Liquidity, And AI.

SA analyst Leo Nelissen outlined: “As we head toward 2026, I think the biggest opportunities and risks lie in some of the market’s most critical areas. This includes the market-leading Mag-7 companies,” in My 3 Big Market Predictions For 2026: The Shift I'm Positioning For Now.

SA analyst Andrew Hecht stated: “The S&P 500 is poised to reach 10,000 by 2026, driven by fiat currency debasement rather than traditional valuation metrics,” in 2026 S&P 500 Outlook: My Target Is 10,000 Due To The Devaluation Of Fiat Currencies. 

SA analyst Eugenio Catone said: “A tightening monetary policy is the biggest enemy for stock market bubbles. The good news is that we are heading in the opposite direction; in fact, the market is pricing in 3–4 cuts of 25 bps each until December 2026,” in The Main Threat For 2026. 

Additional Information

For investors looking to keep a pulse on the broader market’s they can always shift their attention towards exchange traded funds: 

Dow ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (UDOW), (DOG), (DXD), and (SDOW). 

S&P 500 ETFs: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (FXAIX), (VFIAX), (VFFSX), and (SWPPX). 

Nasdaq ETFs: (QQQ), (QLD), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: ASP Isotopes initiated Overweight at Cantor on imminent spinoff value creation
data.description:
ASP Isotopes (ASPI) +17.5% in Thursday's trading as Cantor Fitzgerald initiated coverage with an Overweight rating and $13 price target, believing its wholly-owned Quantum Leap Energy subsidiary will create multiple hundreds of millions of dollars in ASPI shareholder value via an imminent spinoff.

The newly created entity will own ASP Isotopes' (ASPI) quantum enrichment technology, which uses a laser-based enrichment primarily to create high-assay low-enriched uranium, with Cantor analyst Derek Soderberg noting the current HALEU supply chain resides almost exclusively in Russia and his belief that HALEU demand will reach the tens of billions of dollars by the early 2030s, driven by forecasts for small modular reactor growth.

ASP Isotopes (ASPI) looks well-positioned to become a key provider of HALEU, Soderberg wrote, with the first technology validation point supporting his thesis is the company's supply framework agreement with TerraPower, which includes TerraPower funding ASP's first plant buildout, which also includes a ton-scale HALEU plant that will exclusively supply HALEU to TerraPower.

Moreover, through the pending purchase of the Renergen distressed asset, ASP (ASPI) could own the world's largest and highest concentration helium deposit that, according to the company, can supply ~12% of the world's helium from just 4% of the reserve, the analyst said.

data.title: Halozyme granted injunction in Germany barring Merck's subcutaneous Keytruda
data.description:
• A court in Munich has granted a preliminary injunction to Halozyme (HALO) that prevents Merck (MRK) from distributing the subcutaneous version of its cancer treatment Keytruda (pembrolizumab) in Germany.
  • Halozyme previously filed a patent infringement lawsuit against Merck arguing that the SC version violates patents related to its MDASE drug delivery system.
  • German patients will still have access to the original infusion version of Keytruda. 
  • Halozyme has also sued Merck in a New Jersey federal court, alleging that Keytruda Qlex violates 15 MDASE patents.

data.title: Fanuc teams with Nvidia to integrate AI into factories
data.description:
• Japanese industrial machinery company Fanuc (OTCPK:FANUY) announced earlier this month it was teaming with Nvidia (NVDA) to bring artificial intelligence to its factories and production simulations.
  • Fanuc, which makes robot arms, will use Nvidia's robotic simulation framework and incorporate it into its own software. It will also bring together other software makers to work with Nvidia so they're capable of designing robots to better recognize voice commands, avoid human injury and track moving parts.
  • The partnership brings Fanuc deeper into the world of “physical AI,” where machines are capable of making decisions based on data acting upon them instantly. It will also see the two companies build robots that use Nvidia's GPUs, with the robots trained inside Nvidia's virtual factory, part of its “digital twin” initiative. 
  • Nvidia has been pushing deeper into robotics in recent memory and recently announced that its Jetson AGX Thor, AGX Orin and Jetson Orin Nano Super developer kits will be on sale until Jan. 11 to help “give anyone the power to build intelligent robots of the future.”

data.title: Market Voices: Pulte faces GAO probe, Paramount vs. WBD, Ford CEO
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements, and remarks that could impact markets, sectors, or individual stocks. 

  • The Government Accountability Office, which provides auditing and investigative services for Congress, is investigating Federal Housing Finance Authority Director Bill Pulte at the request of Senate Democrats. 

“I can confirm that GAO has accepted this request following our standard process,” a GAO spokesperson told CNBC. 

"The first thing GAO does as any work begins is to determine the full scope of what we will cover and the methodology to be used,” the spokesperson said. “This can take a few months, and until that is done, we cannot provide any estimates on a completion date.”

Pulte has been vocal in calling for the Justice Department to investigate and prosecute several government officials for alleged mortgage fraud, including New York Attorney General Letitia James, Federal Reserve Gov. Lisa Cook, U.S. Sen. Adam Schiff, and U.S. Rep. Eric Swalwell, CNBC noted.

  • Paramount Skydance (PSKY) has reportedly sent a letter to Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) CEO David Zaslav questioning the fairness of WBD's sales process for the company.

“It has become increasingly clear, through media reporting and otherwise, that WBD appears to have abandoned the semblance and reality of a fair transaction process, thereby abdicating its duties to stockholders, and embarked on a myopic process with a predetermined outcome that favors a single bidder,” the letter stated, according to CNBC. “We specifically request and expect this letter will be shared and discussed with the full board of directors of WBD.”

CNBC added that Paramount's letter cited reports that WBD appeared to be favoring a bid by Netflix (NFLX). Paramount, Comcast (CMCSA), and Netflix have all made bids for either some or all of WBD. A second round of bids was submitted earlier this week.

  • Ford (F) CEO Jim Farley said the Trump administration's move to roll back fuel efficiency standards on new cars will help make them more affordable for Americans.

"What you should know is that this is a victory for affordability and common sense. As the president said, we will be able to offer more affordability on our popular models, and we'll be able to launch new vehicles built in America that are more affordable because of this rule change," Ford CEO Jim Farley told Fox News on Thursday. 

Farley added that the standards had been "totally out of touch with the market reality. We were forced to sell EVs and other vehicles. We're not going back to gas-guzzlers."

"We have a lot of EVs and a lot of hybrids at Ford, but now customers get a chance to choose what they want, not by what we force on them," Farley added.

data.title: JAKKS Pacific partners with Hershey for candy-themed Charming line
data.description:
• JAKKS Pacific (JAKK) is partnering with Hershey (HSY) to develop Charming dolls and collectibles inspired by Hershey (HSY) treats.
  • The collection will kick off with the Hershey’s Kisses collection, debuting at CVS (CVS) on December 26 to capitalize on candy sales for Valentine’s Day. The partnership will run through December 2026 with an option for extension.
  • Other collections will be inspired by Hershey (HSY) candy like Jolly Ranchers, Reese’s, Bubble Yum, Twizzlers, and Hershey’s Syrup. Each doll will come with themed charms and accessories.
  • “Partnering with The Hershey Company allows us to blend two worlds that people love, delicious treats and self-expression,” said Debbie Haag, Senior Vice President at JAKKS Pacific.

data.title: SQM downgraded at Goldman as shares run ahead of fundamentals
data.description:
Sociedad Quimica y Minera de Chile (SQM) -4.5% in Thursday's trading after the Chilean lithium company was downgraded to Neutral from Buy with a $63 price target at Goldman Sachs, pointing to lower than expected lithium prices.

Goldman analyst Marcio Farid said his Buy rating on SQM (SQM) had been supported by expectations of a cyclical lithium price rebound and better free cash flow conversion as production grows, but the stock has rallied 80% YTD and 115% since June 2025, which indicates the stock has run ahead of fundamentals.

Farid said SQM (SQM) shares are pricing in a ~$18,000/ton lithium carbonate price, which is 70%-80% above spot and 30% above Goldman's long-term price expectations, as the bank's commodities team sees lithium prices in the $9,250-$11,500/ton range in the next couple of years as strong demand growth in 2026-27 is insufficient to balance a market that is expected to grow by 16%-27% on the supply side.

The analyst also expects SQM (SQM) capex to remain elevated as the company invests in lithium growth in Chile and Australia as well as on iodine capacity expansion, adding these investments should pay off but add pressure to free cash flow generation in 2026-27.

On the positive side, Farid sees solid operational cost and growth performance on lithium in Chile and the ramp-up of Mt. Holland in Australia, as well as strong profitability on Iodine, while SQM's (SQM) "unique position on the cash cost curve and expected production growth should place the company in a privileged position when the lithium market balance is reached/LT prices are achieved," likely not before the end of the decade.

data.title: Google brings Gemini 3 Deep Think model to Gemini app
data.description:
• Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) said on Thursday that it was bringing its advanced Gemini 3 Deep Think model to Google AI subscribers in the Gemini app, allowing users to test its advanced reasoning capabilities.
  • “This new mode delivers a meaningful improvement in reasoning capabilities, designed to tackle complex math, science and logic problems that challenge even the most advanced state-of-the-art models,” Google wrote in a blog post. “Gemini 3 Deep Think excels on rigorous benchmarks like Humanity’s Last Exam and ARC-AGI-2. This is because it uses advanced parallel reasoning to explore multiple hypotheses simultaneously — building on Gemini 2.5 Deep Think variants that recently achieved a gold-medal standard at the International Mathematical Olympiad and at the International Collegiate Programming Contest World Finals.”
  • Google has received wide praise after it released its Gemini 3 model last month. Some analysts have even suggested it derisked the company from the possibility of losing search market share to generative AI competitors.
  • Shares of the tech giant fell fractionally in midday trading on Thursday.

data.title: Target testing overnight delivery with sorting centers and gig drivers -- report
data.description:
Target (TGT) is getting into the overnight delivery arena to stay competitive with rivals Amazon (AMZN) and Walmart (WMT), by establishing sorting centers and using gig workers to complete deliveries.

According to The Wall Street Journal, the retailer has stopped fulfilling ship-to-home orders from some of its busiest stores, opened a new sorting center in Cleveland, and using delivery drivers for online orders in San Diego.

“We believe there's a use case for same-day delivery, there's a use case for drive-up, there's a use case for ship-to-home brown box, and, of course, the bread and butter of our business, there is a use case for people coming into our stores," said Target’s chief supply chain and logistics officer Gretchen McCarthy in an interview with The Wall Street Journal.

Plagued by dwindling traffic to its retail stores, Target (TGT) is adopting a new tactic to boost sales, capitalizing on the increasing popularity of online sales. While comparable store sales were down 2.7% in the latest quarter, online comparable sales rose 2.4% while digital sales were 19% of overall sales, nearly triple from 6 years ago.

As retailers accelerate their ability to deliver in a day or less – with Amazon (AMZN) now aiming for under 30 mins – Target (TGT) has typically relied on retail locations to fulfill online orders. However, this cannibalizes inventory available for shoppers and takes up high-value store space for packing and shipping functions. At the same time, Target (TGT) needs to match the delivery times of its competitors to keep online shoppers from seeking quicker delivery elsewhere.

To achieve this, Target (TGT) opened a 40,000 square foot sortation center in Cleveland that is operated by a third-party logistics provider and is only available to Shipt drivers who are available to pick up orders more frequently than comparable services from UPS (UPS) or FedEx (FDX).

At the same time, in areas without a sortation center, such as San Diego, Target (TGT) is experimenting with Shipt drivers to pick up orders directly from the store for immediate delivery.

During the company’s most recent earnings call with analysts, incoming Target (TGT) CEO Michael Fiddelke laid out the company’s priorities for the future, including an increased effort to “more fully use technology to improve our speed, guest experience and efficiency throughout the business.”

“With our evolving market fulfillment strategy, more than half of the U.S. is eligible for next-day shipping, and we expect to meaningfully expand that reach in the coming year,” Fiddelke said on the call.

data.title: Republican healthcare plan to include pharmacy benefit manager reform - report
data.description:
• A Republican plan to overhaul health care will reportedly include elements aimed at curbing the influence of pharmacy benefit managers as a way to help control drug costs.
  • Politico said that House Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) and other Republican leaders are meeting Thursday to hammer out the details of the plan, which is set to be presented at a Republican conference meeting on Dec. 9.
  • The news site reported that Republicans want to have their own bill to vote on as Democrats continue to apply pressure to extend enhanced ACA health insurance subsidies in to 2026, a provision that some in the GOP support.
  • Separately, Politico reported that Republican strategist and pollster Tony Fabrizio recently told some House Republicans during a closed-door meeting that to counter attacks on the potential ending of subsidies, they should focus on reducing drug prices.  
  • The three major PBMs are UnitedHealth Group's (UNH) OptumRx, Cigna's (CI) Express Scripts, and CVS Health's (CVS) Caremark.
